questions.explanation: For a finance lease, the ROU asset is amortized on a straight-line basis over the lease term (ASC 842-20-35-1). Annual amortization = $200,000 / 5 years = $40,000. Accumulated amortization after 3 years = $40,000 x 3 = $120,000. Net carrying amount at December 31, Year 3 = $200,000 - $120,000 = $80,000.
questions.explanationJa: ファイナンスリースのROU資産はリース期間にわたり定額法で償却する（ASC 842-20-35-1）。年間償却費 = $200,000 ÷ 5年 = $40,000。3年間の累計償却額 = $40,000 × 3 = $120,000。Year 3末の帳簿価額 = $200,000 − $120,000 = $80,000。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Lessee Corp enters into a 3-year operating lease with annual payments of $30,000 due at the BEGINNING of each year. The lessee's incremental borrowing rate is 6%. Using the present value of an annuity-due factor of 2.83339 at 6% for 3 periods, what is the initial lease liability?
questions.explanation: For an annuity due (payments at the beginning of each period), the initial lease liability is calculated using the present value of an annuity-due factor. Lease liability = $30,000 x 2.83339 = $85,002 (rounded). Note that the first payment of $30,000 is made at lease commencement, so immediately after the first payment, the lease liability would be $85,002 - $30,000 = $55,002.
questions.explanationJa: 期首払い（前払い年金）の場合、リース負債の初期測定額は前払い年金現価係数を使用して計算する。リース負債 = $30,000 × 2.83339 = $85,002（四捨五入）。なお、最初の$30,000は開始日に支払われるため、支払直後のリース負債は$85,002 − $30,000 = $55,002となる。

questions.subtopic: Lease with Purchase Option - Lessee Journal Entry
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Lessee Corp enters into a 5-year lease with annual payments of $20,000 (end of year). The lease includes a purchase option at $10,000 that is reasonably certain to be exercised. The implicit rate is 7%. The present value of the 5 annual payments is $82,004, and the present value of the $10,000 purchase option is $7,130. What journal entry should Lessee Corp record at lease commencement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit ROU Asset $82,004; Credit Lease Liability $82,004

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit ROU Asset $89,134; Credit Lease Liability $89,134

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit ROU Asset $82,004; Debit Prepaid Purchase Option $7,130; Credit Lease Liability $89,134

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Equipment $89,134; Credit Lease Liability $89,134

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-30-1, the lease liability includes the present value of all lease payments, which includes the purchase option exercise price when the option is reasonably certain to be exercised. Lease liability = PV of annual payments ($82,004) + PV of purchase option ($7,130) = $89,134. The ROU asset is initially measured at the same amount as the lease liability (assuming no prepaid rent, initial direct costs, or incentives). The entry is: Debit ROU Asset $89,134; Credit Lease Liability $89,134.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-30-1により、リース負債は全てのリース料の現在価値を含み、行使が合理的に確実な購入オプションの行使価格も含む。リース負債 = 年間支払のPV（$82,004）+ 購入オプションのPV（$7,130）= $89,134。ROU資産はリース負債と同額で初期測定する（前払リース料、当初直接コスト、インセンティブがない場合）。仕訳: ROU資産 $89,134（借方）、リース負債 $89,134（貸方）。

questions.stem: Lessee Corp leases retail space with annual base rent of $50,000 plus 3% of gross sales exceeding $1,000,000. In Year 1, Lessee Corp's gross sales are $1,500,000. How should the lessee account for the variable payment?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Include the estimated variable payment of $15,000 in the initial lease liability

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize the $15,000 variable payment as lease expense in Year 1 when incurred

choices.label: C
choices.text: Capitalize the $15,000 as an addition to the ROU asset

choices.label: D
choices.text: Defer the $15,000 and amortize it over the remaining lease term

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-55-1, variable lease payments that depend on sales or usage (performance-based) are NOT included in the measurement of the lease liability because they are not in-substance fixed payments. They are recognized as variable lease expense in the period in which the obligation is incurred. Variable payment = 3% x ($1,500,000 - $1,000,000) = 3% x $500,000 = $15,000, recognized as lease expense in Year 1.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-55-1により、売上や使用量に基づく変動リース料（業績連動型）はリース負債の測定に含めない（実質的な固定支払ではないため）。義務が発生した期間に変動リース費用として認識する。変動支払額 = 3% × ($1,500,000 − $1,000,000) = 3% × $500,000 = $15,000。Year 1のリース費用として認識。

questions.stem: Intermediate Lessor has a 10-year head lease classified as a finance lease (with transfer of ownership). Intermediate Lessor subleases the same asset to a third party for 8 years. What is the appropriate classification reference for the sublease?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The sublease is classified by reference to the underlying asset because the head lease transfers ownership

choices.label: B
choices.text: The sublease is classified by reference to the ROU asset from the head lease

choices.label: C
choices.text: The sublease must always be classified as an operating lease

choices.label: D
choices.text: The sublease classification follows the head lease classification automatically

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-25-2(b), a sublease is generally classified by reference to the ROU asset arising from the head lease. However, if the head lease is a finance lease that transfers ownership or contains a purchase option reasonably certain to be exercised, the sublessor classifies the sublease by reference to the underlying asset itself (not the ROU asset). This is because the sublessor effectively controls the underlying asset, not merely a right-of-use.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-25-2(b)により、サブリースは通常ヘッドリースから生じるROU資産を基準に分類する。ただし、ヘッドリースが所有権移転型ファイナンスリース、または行使が合理的に確実な購入オプションを含む場合、転貸人はROU資産ではなく原資産そのものを基準にサブリースを分類する。転貸人が使用権だけでなく原資産を実質的に支配しているためである。

questions.stem: Seller-Lessee Corp sells a building (carrying amount $500,000, fair value $800,000) to Buyer-Lessor Corp and leases it back for 5 years. The leaseback qualifies as a sale under ASC 606. Annual lease payments consist of a fixed component of $60,000 and a variable component based on 2% of annual revenues. How does the variable payment component affect the sale-leaseback accounting?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Variable payments are included in the lease liability measurement

choices.label: B
choices.text: Variable payments are excluded from the lease liability and do not affect the gain calculation

choices.label: C
choices.text: Variable payments are capitalized as part of the ROU asset

choices.label: D
choices.text: Variable payments cause the transaction to fail sale recognition

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-40-25-1, in a sale-leaseback where the transfer qualifies as a sale, the seller-lessee recognizes a gain only on the amount of the rights transferred. Variable payments based on revenues are not included in the measurement of the lease liability (ASC 842-20-30-5) because they depend on future performance. Therefore, they do not affect the calculation of the financing obligation or the gain recognized on the sale. They are expensed as incurred in future periods.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-40-25-1により、売却が認められるセール・リースバックでは、売手＝借手は移転した権利に対応する部分のみ利益を認識する。収益に基づく変動支払はリース負債の測定に含まれず（ASC 842-20-30-5）、将来の業績に依存するため、金融負債や売却利益の計算に影響しない。発生時に費用処理する。

questions.stem: Lessor reimburses Lessee $50,000 for leasehold improvements at the commencement of a 10-year operating lease. The lease has annual payments of $120,000 (end of year), and the incremental borrowing rate is 5%. The present value of the lease payments is $926,399. What is the initial ROU asset?
questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-30-5, the ROU asset is initially measured at the amount of the lease liability, plus any prepaid lease payments and initial direct costs, minus any lease incentives received. The lessor's reimbursement of $50,000 for leasehold improvements is a lease incentive under ASC 842-10-30-2. Initial ROU asset = $926,399 (lease liability) - $50,000 (lease incentive) = $876,399.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-30-5により、ROU資産の初期測定額 = リース負債 + 前払リース料 + 当初直接コスト − リースインセンティブ。貸手による内装工事$50,000の償還はASC 842-10-30-2に基づくリースインセンティブ。初期ROU資産 = $926,399（リース負債）− $50,000（リースインセンティブ）= $876,399。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Lessee Corp enters into a 10-month lease for equipment and elects the short-term lease exemption. At month 8, the lease is modified to extend the term by an additional 6 months. What is the accounting impact of this modification?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Continue the short-term lease exemption because the original term was 10 months

choices.label: B
choices.text: Recognize a lease liability and ROU asset at the modification date because the remaining term now exceeds 12 months from commencement

choices.label: C
choices.text: Recognize a lease liability and ROU asset at the modification date because the total lease term now exceeds 12 months

choices.label: D
choices.text: Continue expensing as a short-term lease because the extension was not part of the original contract

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-25-2, the short-term lease exemption applies to leases with a term of 12 months or less at commencement. When a lease modification extends the total term beyond 12 months (10 months + 6 months = 16 months), the lease no longer qualifies as short-term. At the modification date, the lessee must recognize a lease liability and ROU asset for the modified lease based on the remaining lease term and payments, using a discount rate at the modification date.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-25-2により、短期リースの免除は開始時にリース期間が12ヶ月以下のリースに適用される。リース変更により総期間が12ヶ月を超える場合（10ヶ月 + 6ヶ月 = 16ヶ月）、短期リースの要件を満たさなくなる。変更日に残存リース期間と支払額に基づき、変更日の割引率を使用してリース負債とROU資産を認識する。

questions.stem: Lessor Corp enters into a sales-type lease on January 1, Year 1. The equipment has a cost of $60,000 and a fair value of $85,000. The lease term is 5 years with annual payments of $20,000 at year-end. The implicit rate is 5.67%. When does Lessor Corp recognize the selling profit?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Ratably over the 5-year lease term

choices.label: B
choices.text: At lease commencement, recognizing the full selling profit of $25,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: At the end of the lease term when the asset is transferred

choices.label: D
choices.text: In proportion to lease payments received each year

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-30-25-1, for a sales-type lease, the lessor recognizes selling profit (or loss) at the commencement date. The lessor derecognizes the underlying asset and recognizes a net investment in the lease. Selling profit = Fair value of asset - Carrying amount = $85,000 - $60,000 = $25,000. This is recognized entirely at commencement, similar to a sale. The lessor also recognizes interest income over the lease term on the net investment.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-30-25-1により、セールス型リースでは貸手は開始日に売却損益を認識する。原資産を認識中止し、リースの純投資を認識する。売却利益 = 公正価値 − 帳簿価額 = $85,000 − $60,000 = $25,000。これは売却と同様に開始時に全額認識する。貸手はリース期間にわたり純投資に対する利息収益も認識する。

questions.stem: Lessor Corp enters into a direct financing lease on January 1, Year 1. The net investment in the lease at commencement is $100,000, consisting of lease receivable of $85,000 and unguaranteed residual asset of $15,000. The implicit rate is 6%. What is the interest income recognized in Year 1, and how does the residual asset change?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Interest income of $5,100 on the receivable only; residual asset remains at $15,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Interest income of $6,000 on the total net investment; residual asset accretes

choices.label: C
choices.text: Interest income of $5,100; residual asset is amortized to zero

choices.label: D
choices.text: Interest income of $6,000 on the receivable only; residual asset is written off

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-30-35-4, the lessor recognizes interest income on the net investment in the lease as a whole. Interest income in Year 1 = $100,000 x 6% = $6,000. The net investment includes both the lease receivable and the unguaranteed residual asset. The residual asset accretes (increases) over the lease term as part of the unwinding of the discount on the net investment. The interest income is allocated between the receivable (reduced by cash received) and the residual asset (which grows over time to its expected terminal value).
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-30-35-4により、貸手はリースの純投資全体に対して利息収益を認識する。Year 1の利息収益 = $100,000 × 6% = $6,000。純投資にはリース債権と無保証残存資産の両方が含まれる。残存資産は純投資の割引の解消（時間の経過）によりリース期間にわたり増価する。利息収益は債権（現金受領により減少）と残存資産（時間とともに期待最終価値まで増加）に配分される。

questions.subtopic: Finance Lease vs Operating Lease - Income Statement Impact
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Lessee Corp enters into a 4-year lease with annual payments of $40,000 at year-end. The lease liability is $135,268 (IBR = 7%). Under which classification would total expense be higher in Year 1?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Finance lease, because it front-loads interest expense

choices.label: B
choices.text: Operating lease, because straight-line expense always exceeds finance lease expense

choices.label: C
choices.text: Total expense is the same in Year 1 under both classifications

choices.label: D
choices.text: It depends on whether a purchase option exists

questions.explanation: For a finance lease, Year 1 total expense = amortization ($135,268 / 4 = $33,817) + interest ($135,268 x 7% = $9,469) = $43,286. For an operating lease, straight-line expense = total payments / lease term = ($40,000 x 4) / 4 = $40,000 per year. Finance lease Year 1 expense ($43,286) exceeds operating lease expense ($40,000) because finance lease accounting front-loads the interest component. Over the full lease term, total expense is the same ($160,000) under both methods.
questions.explanationJa: ファイナンスリースのYear 1総費用 = 償却費（$135,268 ÷ 4 = $33,817）+ 利息（$135,268 × 7% = $9,469）= $43,286。オペレーティングリースの定額費用 = 支払総額 ÷ リース期間 = ($40,000 × 4) ÷ 4 = $40,000/年。ファイナンスリースのYear 1費用（$43,286）はオペレーティングリース（$40,000）を上回る。利息が前倒しで計上されるためである。リース期間全体の総費用は両方式とも同額（$160,000）。

questions.subtopic: Lease Component - Non-Lease Component Practical Expedient
questions.stem: Under ASC 842, a lessee enters into a contract that includes a lease of office equipment and maintenance services. The lessee elects the practical expedient to not separate lease and non-lease components. How should the lessee account for the combined payments?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Allocate payments between the lease and non-lease components based on relative stand-alone prices

choices.label: B
choices.text: Treat the entire contract consideration as a single lease component

choices.label: C
choices.text: Treat the entire contract consideration as a service expense

choices.label: D
choices.text: Capitalize only the lease portion and expense the remainder

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-15-37, as a practical expedient, a lessee may elect by class of underlying asset not to separate non-lease components from lease components. When this expedient is elected, the lessee accounts for each lease component and the associated non-lease components as a single lease component. This means the entire contract consideration (including maintenance) is included in the measurement of the lease liability and ROU asset.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-15-37により、借手は原資産のクラスごとに非リース構成要素をリース構成要素から分離しない実務上の便法を選択できる。この便法を選択すると、各リース構成要素と関連する非リース構成要素を単一のリース構成要素として処理する。メンテナンスを含む契約対価全体がリース負債とROU資産の測定に含まれる。

questions.stem: Lessee Corp terminates an operating lease early. At the termination date, the ROU asset has a carrying amount of $45,000 and the lease liability has a balance of $50,000. Lessee Corp pays a $5,000 termination penalty. What journal entry should Lessee Corp record?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Debit Lease Liability $50,000, Credit ROU Asset $45,000, Credit Cash $5,000

choices.label: B
choices.text: Debit Lease Liability $50,000, Debit Loss $5,000, Credit ROU Asset $45,000, Credit Cash $10,000

choices.label: C
choices.text: Debit Lease Liability $50,000, Debit Termination Expense $5,000, Credit ROU Asset $45,000, Credit Cash $5,000, Credit Gain $5,000

choices.label: D
choices.text: Debit Lease Liability $50,000, Credit ROU Asset $45,000, Credit Cash $5,000, no gain or loss

questions.explanation: Upon early termination of a lease, the lessee derecognizes the ROU asset and lease liability. The termination penalty is paid in cash. Journal entry: Debit Lease Liability $50,000, Credit ROU Asset $45,000, Credit Cash $5,000. The difference between the lease liability ($50,000) and the ROU asset ($45,000) is $5,000, which exactly offsets the termination penalty paid. In this case, net gain or loss = Lease liability removed ($50,000) - ROU asset removed ($45,000) - Penalty paid ($5,000) = $0. No separate gain or loss is recognized.
questions.explanationJa: リースの早期解約時、借手はROU資産とリース負債を認識中止する。解約違約金は現金で支払う。仕訳: リース負債 $50,000（借方）、ROU資産 $45,000（貸方）、現金 $5,000（貸方）。リース負債（$50,000）とROU資産（$45,000）の差額$5,000が解約違約金$5,000と相殺される。純損益 = 除去リース負債$50,000 − 除去ROU資産$45,000 − 違約金$5,000 = $0。

questions.stem: Under ASC 842, what is the distinction between the lease inception date and the lease commencement date?

choices.label: A
choices.text: They are the same date; both refer to when the lessee begins using the asset

choices.label: B
choices.text: Inception is when the lease agreement is signed or committed to; commencement is when the lessor makes the asset available to the lessee

choices.label: C
choices.text: Inception is when the first payment is made; commencement is when the lease term begins

choices.label: D
choices.text: Inception is when the asset is delivered; commencement is when lease payments begin

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-20, the inception date is the earlier of the date of the lease agreement or the date of commitment by the parties to the principal terms and conditions of the lease. The commencement date is the date on which a lessor makes an underlying asset available for use by a lessee. Lease classification is assessed at inception, but measurement of the lease liability and ROU asset occurs at commencement.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-20により、開始日（inception）はリース契約日または当事者がリースの主要条件にコミットした日の早い方。使用開始日（commencement）は貸手が原資産を借手の使用に供する日。リース分類は開始日に評価されるが、リース負債とROU資産の測定は使用開始日に行われる。

questions.stem: Parent Co leases equipment to Subsidiary Co at an annual rental of $5,000 for 5 years. The market rate for a similar lease is $15,000 per year. Under ASC 842, how should the subsidiary account for this below-market lease in its separate financial statements?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Record the lease at the market rate of $15,000 per year and recognize the difference as a capital contribution

choices.label: B
choices.text: Record the lease based on the legally enforceable terms of $5,000 per year

choices.label: C
choices.text: Record the lease at the market rate and recognize the difference as a gain

choices.label: D
choices.text: Do not record any lease because the terms are not at arm's length

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-55-12, related-party leases are classified and accounted for based on the legally enforceable terms and conditions, not the economic substance or fair market value. The subsidiary records the lease at $5,000 per year as stated in the agreement. The difference from market rates is not separately recognized in the lessee's separate financial statements. Consolidated financial statements would eliminate the intercompany lease.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-55-12により、関連当事者間リースは経済的実態や公正市場価値ではなく、法的に執行可能な条件に基づいて分類・会計処理する。子会社は契約に記載された年間$5,000でリースを記録する。市場レートとの差額は借手の個別財務諸表では別途認識しない。連結財務諸表ではグループ内リースは相殺消去される。

questions.stem: Lessee Corp enters into a 5-year office lease with three consecutive 3-year renewal options. The annual rent is $100,000 during the initial term and increases by 5% for each renewal. Lessee Corp has made significant leasehold improvements and has no viable relocation alternatives. What lease term should be used for measurement?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 5 years (initial term only)

choices.label: B
choices.text: 8 years (initial term plus first renewal)

choices.label: C
choices.text: 14 years (initial term plus all three renewals)

choices.label: D
choices.text: 11 years (initial term plus first two renewals)

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-55-26, factors such as significant leasehold improvements with remaining economic life beyond the current term and the lack of viable relocation alternatives create strong economic incentives to exercise renewal options. Both factors indicate that ALL three renewal options are reasonably certain to be exercised. Therefore, the lease term includes the initial 5 years plus all three 3-year renewal periods: 5 + 3 + 3 + 3 = 14 years.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-55-26により、現行期間を超える耐用年数を持つ大規模内装工事と、代替的な移転先がないことは、更新オプション行使の強い経済的インセンティブを生む。両要因により3つの更新オプション全ての行使が合理的に確実である。リース期間 = 当初5年 + 更新3回（3年×3）= 14年。

questions.stem: A company has three operating leases with the following remaining terms and lease liabilities: Lease A (4 years, $100,000), Lease B (8 years, $200,000), Lease C (2 years, $100,000). What is the weighted average remaining lease term for disclosure purposes?

choices.label: A
choices.text: 4.7 years

choices.label: B
choices.text: 5.5 years

choices.label: C
choices.text: 6.0 years

choices.label: D
choices.text: 4.0 years

questions.explanation: The weighted average remaining lease term is calculated by weighting each lease's remaining term by its lease liability. Total lease liability = $100,000 + $200,000 + $100,000 = $400,000. Weighted average = (4 x $100,000 + 8 x $200,000 + 2 x $100,000) / $400,000 = ($400,000 + $1,600,000 + $200,000) / $400,000 = $2,200,000 / $400,000 = 5.5 years. The simple average would be (4 + 8 + 2) / 3 = 4.67 years, but the weighted average gives a higher result because Lease B (the longest) has the largest liability.

questions.stem: Under ASC 842-20-50-4, which of the following is a required quantitative disclosure for lessees?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The lessor's implicit rate for each individual lease

choices.label: B
choices.text: The weighted average discount rate for operating and finance leases, presented separately

choices.label: C
choices.text: The risk-free rate used for all leases

choices.label: D
choices.text: Only the incremental borrowing rate for the largest lease

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-20-50-4(g)(2), lessees are required to disclose the weighted average discount rate for finance leases and operating leases, separately. This provides users of financial statements with information about the rates used to measure lease liabilities. The disclosure is the weighted average, not individual rates, and it must distinguish between finance and operating leases.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-20-50-4(g)(2)により、借手はファイナンスリースとオペレーティングリースの加重平均割引率を区分して開示する必要がある。これにより財務諸表利用者にリース負債の測定に使用した割引率の情報を提供する。個別のレートではなく加重平均であり、ファイナンスリースとオペレーティングリースを区別する必要がある。

questions.stem: Lessor Corp leases equipment to a lessee with questionable creditworthiness. The lease meets the transfer of ownership criterion. However, it is not probable that the lessor will collect the lease payments. How should the lessor classify this lease?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Sales-type lease, because the transfer of ownership criterion is met

choices.label: B
choices.text: Direct financing lease, with an allowance for doubtful accounts

choices.label: C
choices.text: Operating lease, because collectibility is not probable

choices.label: D
choices.text: No lease is recognized until payments are received

questions.explanation: Under ASC 842-10-25-3(b)(1), even if a lease meets one of the five classification criteria (such as transfer of ownership), the lessor classifies the lease as an operating lease if it is not probable that the lessor will collect the lease payments plus any amount necessary to satisfy a residual value guarantee. Collectibility must be probable for sales-type or direct financing classification. When collectibility is not probable, the lease is classified as an operating lease.
questions.explanationJa: ASC 842-10-25-3(b)(1)により、5つの分類基準（所有権移転等）を満たしても、貸手がリース料と残存価額保証額の回収が見込めない場合、オペレーティングリースに分類する。セールス型または直接金融型に分類するには回収可能性が見込まれる（probable）必要がある。回収可能性が見込まれない場合はオペレーティングリースとなる。

questions.subtopic: Finance Lease - Total Lease Cost Over Term
questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Lessee Corp enters into a 3-year finance lease with annual payments of $50,000 due at the end of each year. The implicit rate is 6%, and the present value of the lease payments is $133,651. The equipment has no residual value. What is the total lease cost (amortization plus interest) over the entire 3-year term?
questions.explanation: For a finance lease, total lease cost over the entire term equals total cash payments. Total amortization of the ROU asset = $133,651 (the full asset is amortized). Total interest expense = total payments - principal = $150,000 - $133,651 = $16,349. Total lease cost = amortization ($133,651) + interest ($16,349) = $150,000, which equals total cash payments ($50,000 x 3). This is the same total as an operating lease; the difference is only in the timing pattern.
questions.explanationJa: ファイナンスリースのリース期間全体の総リースコストは現金支払総額と等しい。ROU資産の総償却額 = $133,651（全額償却）。利息費用の合計 = 支払総額 − 元本 = $150,000 − $133,651 = $16,349。総リースコスト = 償却費（$133,651）+ 利息（$16,349）= $150,000 = 現金支払総額（$50,000 × 3）。オペレーティングリースと総額は同じで、タイミングパターンのみ異なる。

questions.stem: On January 1, Year 1, Lessee Corp enters into a 4-year operating lease with annual payments of $30,000 due at the end of each year. The incremental borrowing rate is 5%. The present value of the lease payments is $106,380 (PV annuity factor at 5% for 4 years = 3.54595). What is the operating lease liability balance at December 31, Year 2?
questions.explanation: The lease liability is amortized using the effective interest method. Year 1: Beginning balance = $106,380. Interest = $106,380 x 5% = $5,319. Payment = $30,000. Ending balance = $106,380 + $5,319 - $30,000 = $81,699. Year 2: Beginning balance = $81,699. Interest = $81,699 x 5% = $4,085. Payment = $30,000. Ending balance = $81,699 + $4,085 - $30,000 = $55,784. Note that choice B ($81,699) is the balance at the end of Year 1, not Year 2.
